channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Lawmakers, legal experts warn shuttering USAID is unconstitutional
item.description: Lawmakers and legal experts are warning the Trump administration’s efforts to shutter the government’s foreign assistance agency is unconstitutional and is likely to face challenges in court. Over the weekend, agents of the so-called Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) entered the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, The Hill

item.title: Five takeaways on Musk, Trump war with USAID
item.description: President Trump and billionaire tech entrepreneur Elon Musk have moved to shut down the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), which spend some $30 billion last year on humanitarian, development and security aid to more than 100 countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Trump pauses Canadian tariffs for 30 days in deal with Trudeau
item.description: President Trump and Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau agreed to a pause in implementing tariffs for at least a month, just hours after Trump agreed to delay tariffs on Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Democrat places blanket hold on Trump’s State Department picks over targeting of USAID
item.description: Sen. Brian Schatz (D-Hawaii), a member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, announced Monday he will place a blanket hold on all of President Trump’s nominees to the State Department until the president backs off his effort to shut down USAID, the nation’s chief foreign assistance agency.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Emergency crews deployed on Santorini as an earthquake swarm worries Greek experts
item.description: SANTORINI, Greece (AP) — Authorities deployed emergency rescue crews to Greece's volcanic island of Santorini on Monday as hundreds of people scrambled to leave after a spike in seismic activity raised concerns about a potentially powerful earthquake.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ontario will end provincial contract with Musk's Starlink amid Trump tariffs
item.description: Ontario’s premier said he’s “ripping up” the contract between his province and Elon Musk’s Starlink internet service, following President Trump’s decision to target Canada with 25 percent tariffs, which are set to go into effect Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: How Trump's pledge to punish South Africa reflects Musk's criticism of his homeland
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump says he will cut all funding to South Africa over what he called a massive human rights violation.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In Kentucky bourbon country, the prospect of a trade war feels like a hangover that won't go away
item.description: In the rolling hills of Kentucky where most of the world’s bourbon supply is crafted, the prospect of a new trade war feels like an aching hangover that won’t go away.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schreiner, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tariff threats take aim at fentanyl trafficking. Here's how the drug reaches the US
item.description: President Donald Trump's threat of tariffs on goods from Mexico, Canada and China is aimed at stopping the flow of fentanyl into the U.S. Ingredients for fentanyl are produced in China and used by pharmaceutical companies to make legal painkillers.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Another car bomb in a northern Syria city kills at least 19, mostly women
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Another car bomb exploded on the outskirts of a city in northern Syria on Monday, killing at least 19 people and leaving over a dozen wounded, hospital workers and emergency responders said.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Marco Rubio taking over as acting head of USAID amid agency turmoil
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is being put in charge of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) as the Trump administration mulls the future of the agency.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: A blast in Moscow kills 2, including the founder of a unit fighting in eastern Ukraine, reports say
item.description: An explosion rocked an upscale residential block in Moscow early Monday, killing two people – including the founder of a battalion that battled Kyiv’s forces in eastern Ukraine -- and injuring three others, Russian authorities and news reports said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Africa hits back at Trump’s claim that it is ‘confiscating land,' as US aid to country threatened
item.description: The South African president has hit back at President Donald Trump for claiming the government is "confiscating" land. President Trump also claimed certain classes of people are being treated ‘very badly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Their sacred land was a gift for their courage. Yet Maká people in Paraguay fight for its ownership
item.description: ASUNCIÓN, Paraguay (AP) — Many Maká traditions have slowly faded. Yet a few elders among these Paraguayan Indigenous people recall how their songs imitated birds.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hospitals in eastern Congo are crowded with wounded and exhausting their supplies
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Hundreds of wounded people have poured into overcrowded hospitals in Goma, a major city in eastern Congo, as fighting rages on between government forces and the Rwanda-backed rebels who seized the city of around 2 million people.
item.dc:creator: VIVIEN LATOUR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First Trump-Netanyahu meeting: 3 top issues
item.description: President Trump will welcome Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to the White House this week, with tensions between the two leaders standing in stark contrast to the priority both countries place on the bilateral relationship.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Preoccupied with its troops in Russia, North Korea unlikely to embrace Trump's overture soon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In its first direct criticism of the Trump administration, North Korea lashed out at U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio for calling it a “rogue” state and warned Monday that such “coarse and nonsensical remarks” will never contribute to U.S. interests.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Belgium has a new prime minister, one who long tried to gut the nation and seek regional autonomy
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Belgium has a new prime minister whose political purpose was long to break up the nation, gut the state structures and give ever more autonomy to his northern Flanders at the cost of everyone else.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to speak with Trudeau, Mexico after imposing tariffs
item.description: President Trump said he would speak Monday morning with the leaders of Canada and Mexico after imposing a fresh round of tariffs on imports from those countries and made clear it would take significant action to lift the tariffs. “They have to balance out their trade, No. 1.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Jailed ex-Pakistani PM Imran Khan draws parallels to President Trump in fight for justice
item.description: Former Pakistani Prime Minister Imran Khan looms large over the country's political scene, despite being jailed. Now questions arise as to whether President Trump could bring him some relief.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu departs for US to meet with Trump, hoping to strengthen ties with Washington
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu left for the U.S. on Sunday to meet with U.S. President Donald Trump, looking to strengthen ties with Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio visiting Central America with the Panama Canal and immigration top of mind
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on his first foreign trip in office, arriving in Central America on Saturday to press President Donald Trump's top priority — curbing illegal immigration — and to bring the message that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: King Charles says 'our hearts' are with United States after 'devastating loss of life' in DC plane crash
item.description: King Charles III said Saturday he was "shocked and saddened" about the deadly midair collision near Reagan National Airport Wednesday, saying his thoughts were with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza ceasefire sees its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The ceasefire in Gaza saw its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners on Saturday, and the crucial Rafah border crossing reopened two days before discussions on the truce’s far more difficult second phase begin.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and IMAD ISSEID, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Italy takes back 43 migrants it planned to process in Albania in 3rd failed attempt to keep them out
item.description: SHENGJIN, Albania (AP) — An Italian navy ship on Saturday took migrants to Italy from asylum processing centers in Albania following a court decision in Rome. It was the third failed attempt by Giorgia Meloni’s far-right government to process migrants in the non-EU country.
item.dc:creator: ERION XHABAFTI and LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuela frees 6 Americans after meeting between President Maduro and Trump's envoy
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Six Americans who had been detained in Venezuela in recent months were freed by the government of President Nicolás Maduro after he met Friday with a Trump administration official tasked with urging the authoritarian leader to take back deported migrants who have committed
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 18 Pakistani soldiers killed in fighting with separatist rebels in Balochistan
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani troops fought separatist insurgents who set up roadblocks in the restive northwestern region of Balochistan, leaving 18 paramilitary security forces and 23 rebels dead in some of the heaviest clashes in recent years, officials said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian drone and missile attacks kill 8 in Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — At least eight people died overnight as Russian drone and missile strikes pounded Ukraine’s towns and cities, local officials said Saturday. Meanwhile, Moscow’s troops continued their grinding advance through the country’s east.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio heads to Panama, Latin America to pursue Trump's 'Golden Age' agenda
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is set to visit Panama in his first official visit abroad as part of what the State Department said is to underscore President Donald Trump's vision of re-establishing a "Golden Era.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As M23 rebel group advances in Congo, a new leader signals a shift in its identity
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — After Rwanda-backed M23 rebels took control of the biggest city in eastern Congo this week, the man who emerged from the shadows to assert his leadership was not the group's long-time military leader. Sultani Makenga, an ethnic Tutsi rebel leader sanctioned by both the U.S.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Peace prospects look bleak in Myanmar as a civil war rages
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Peace prospects look bleak in Myanmar as a civil war rages despite international pressure on the military four years after it seized power from an elected civilian government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First American hostage to be released from Gaza, Hamas says
item.description: Keith Segel, an American-Israeli in Gaza and the first of American citizens taken on Oct. 7, will be released on Saturday as part of the Israel-Hamas ceasfire deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moment IDF soldier is reunited with family seen on video
item.description: Agam Berger, an IDF soldier who was taken hostage during Hamas' attacks on Oct. 7, 2023, is reunited with her family after spending 482 days in captivity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Year of the Snake is underway with Lunar New Year festivities around the world
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Firecrackers popped, incense was offered at temples and dancers and drummers paraded Wednesday in Asia and farther afield as millions around the world celebrated the Lunar New Year.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately, upending expectations of an extended withdrawal
item.description: President Donald Trump last week issued an executive order to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S. from WHO, but that did not take immediate effect.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, NewsNation
